name: book-markdown

applies_to.description: Manuscript project processed by the BOOK workflow family. Chapters are organized under chapters/CH_*.md, with front matter in front/, back matter in back/, draft material in drafts/, and RDC source documents in source/. BOOK workflow artefacts (BOOK_MANIFEST.json, STRUCTURE.md, MASTER.md, etc.) are load-bearing and must not be moved. Output from LULU_PIPELINE lives in output/ and is ignored by ORGANIZE.

default_rules.note: BOOK workflow primary output: chapter files in chapters/ following CH_<N>_<title>.md naming are at their canonical location and must not be moved
BOOK workflow manifest and spec files are load-bearing at project root; BOOK_COMPILER reads them from root; moving them breaks the pipeline
BOOK and RDC workflow artefacts are load-bearing at project root; these files are produced and consumed by the workflow system and must remain where they are
Front matter files (dedication, preface, foreword, acknowledgements, etc.) under front/ are at their canonical location
Back matter files (appendices, bibliography, index, colophon, etc.) under back/ are at their canonical location
Draft material under drafts/ is intentionally staged there — do not move; authors keep drafts/ as a working area separate from committed chapters
RDC source documents under source/ are at their canonical location; source/ feeds RDC_WORKFLOW and should not be reorganized by ORGANIZE
Figures (images, diagrams, charts) under figures/ are at their canonical location; BOOK workflow references them by relative path from root
Temporary scratch markdown files at any depth are candidates for deletion; quarantine to .delete — always ASK_USER before ratifying; book authors may have notes they intend to keep

Numbered note files, old design docs, and version-stamped markdown files are historical; ASK_USER — book authors often have strong attachment to specific notes, so .archive is the correct destination but must not be automatic

Loose markdown at project root not matched by earlier rules — ASK_USER always; a root-level .md in a book project could be a draft chapter, a historical design note, a stray authorial memo, or an intentional orientation file; authorial judgment is required and ORGANIZE must not auto-route these

notes: Book manuscript template aligned with the BOOK workflow family's project layout (see BOOK_WORKFLOW_DISSERTATION.md §14 for the canonical project structure). Key invariant: BOOK, RDC, and SDLC artefacts named in rule t3 are load-bearing workflow files — ORGANIZE must never propose moving or archiving them. Rule t11 (loose *.md at root → ASK_USER) is intentionally conservative: book projects accumulate root-level markdown for many legitimate reasons and authorial judgment is always required. The output/ directory is ignored entirely because LULU_PIPELINE generates it from the source and it is recreated on demand. Flag t8 covers all figure types (PNG, SVG, PDF embedded as figures, etc.) because the glob pattern figures/** matches any file extension.
